meta.title: The Pier at Low Tide
meta.premise: The harbormaster is dead in the water. Five people were on the pier before dawn. Each one has a story.
meta.tone: fogbound coastal noir, salt and secrets, clipped and atmospheric
meta.artStyle: Coastal noir character portrait, head-and-shoulders, fog and salt air palette (slate blue, wet rope brown, gull grey, lantern amber), painted illustration with soft edges, same cold harbor lighting for every cast member, early 20th-century working waterfront fashion, mystery game NPC portrait, consistent artist hand
meta.setting: Coldharbor pier at first light. Fog, low tide, mudflats under the planks. The road and telegraph are down.
meta.summary: Harbormaster Tom Rourke was hauled up dripping from under the pier just after first light — not a simple drowning. Five people admit they were on the pier or aboard boats before dawn: fishmonger Nell Avery, Captain Harlan Briggs, deckhand Jess Pike, summer visitor Dr. Miriam Cole, and night watch Sam Quinn. You are the coastal inspector from the county seat. Until the tide turns and the county boat can reach you, the truth stays between these pilings.
meta.theMystery: Who killed Harbormaster Tom Rourke on the pier before dawn — and what secret was worth a life at low tide?

player.role: Coastal inspector sent down from the county seat
player.clothing: Oilskin over a dark suit, salt already on the cuffs
player.background: County man, not a local — brought in when a body hits the water and the fog cuts the telegraph.
player.publicPerception: Outsider with a badge. Fishermen may close ranks; the watch must answer him.
player.performanceNotes: Same recurring inspector as other cases when personaId matches. Locals may not know his full reputation yet.
player.startingKnowledge: Harbormaster Tom Rourke was found floating under the pier just after first light. The tide was still low. Five people admit they were on the pier or aboard boats before dawn: Nell Avery the fishmonger, Captain Harlan Briggs, deckhand Jess Pike, Dr. Miriam Cole (summer visitor), and watchman Sam Quinn. The road out of town is fog-bound; the telegraph is down. You have until the tide turns and the county boat can reach you.
player.objective: Examine the body and the pier, interview everyone who was there before dawn, follow the maritime paper trail, and accuse the killer with method and motive before high water.

openingNarration: Fog hangs like wet wool over Coldharbor. The pier creaks under your boots. Below the planks, black water shows mudflats and kelp. A tarpaulin covers what used to be Tom Rourke — hauled up an hour ago, still dripping. Lantern light pools on wet wood. Nell Avery stands by her fish shed with her arms folded. Further out, a fishing boat rocks against the piles. Sam Quinn waits by the harbormaster’s shack with a face like a closed door. You are the county inspector. The road is fog-bound, the telegraph is down, and five people were here before dawn. One of them killed the harbormaster. Find who — and why — before the tide turns.

wrapUp.performanceNotes: Fog, tide, and working-waterfront aftermath. The guilty may confess or curse the tide. Keep it salty and grounded.

flags.description: Found bloody gaff on Briggs's boat

locations.name: Coldharbor Pier — the deck
locations.description: Wet planks, rope coils, lanterns bleeding into fog. The ladder down to the water is slick. A tarpaulin covers Tom Rourke's body near the middle piles. The harbormaster's shack stands at the shore end. Nell's fish shed is to the left. Briggs's boat is moored further out.

exits.label: along the outer walk into the fog
down the slick ladder toward the water

hazards.description: The ladder rungs are slick with tide and algae. A careless climb can put you in the black water under the pier.

onInspect.narrativeHints: Rourke is cold and wet. A deep gash at the temple — not a drowning mark. His pocket watch is smashed, hands stopped at 4:12. Brackish water in his lungs means he went in alive or nearly so after the blow. Kelp tangled in his coat, not boat grease.
Rungs wet from the tide. A scrape of dark wood varnish on the third rung down — as if a long pole or gaff was dragged.
The post lantern burned low overnight. Glass is intact. Someone stood here long enough to leave a circle of boot scrapes in the salt.

locations.name: Harbormaster's shack
locations.description: A cramped office of charts, tide tables, and the smell of pipe tobacco. Rourke's desk faces the window onto the pier. A logbook lies open — a page roughly torn out.

onInspect.narrativeHints: Last night's low tide bottomed around 4:00 a.m. High water is due late morning. Bodies and evidence on the flats will be underwater again soon.

Yesterday's entries note boat traffic. A jagged tear removes last night's page. In the desk drawer underneath: the torn scrap, crumpled, still readable — Rourke's hand: 'B. midnight run confirmed. Tell county if he lies again.'
Pipe cold. Ashtray clean. Rourke did not sit and smoke through the night — he went out.

locations.name: Nell's fish shed
locations.description: Ice, knives, and the honest stink of the trade. Nell keeps her books better than half the captains. A kettle steams on a spirit stove.

onInspect.narrativeHints: She bought from Briggs last week. Numbers are clean. A note in the margin: 'Tom in a mood about the night boats again.'
All her knives accounted for. Clean. Whatever killed Rourke did not come from this shed.

locations.name: The Merrow — Briggs's boat
locations.description: A working trawler painted grey and black. Deck wet, gear stowed. A canvas tarp covers something by the port rail. Below, the cabin smells of diesel and old coffee.

onInspect.narrativeHints: Under the tarp: a long-handled gaff. The iron hook is rinsed but not well — a dark crust in the crease. Salt water and blood do not lie the same way. Handle wet above the waterline mark.
Briggs claims he slept aboard. The cabin bunk is made. A lantern was moved recently — soot smear on a bulkhead at standing height, not sleeping height.
Size eleven, still damp with pier mud, not only deck brine. Matching the scrapes near the ladder.

locations.name: Outer walk — fog end
locations.description: Planks vanish into white. Some of the boards flex underfoot — soft with rot where the fog never dries. Somewhere a bell buoy clangs. Dr. Cole stands with a coat too fine for this weather, staring where the bay should be. Jess Pike paces, hands in pockets, jaw set.

exits.label: over the edge into the black water (not a good idea)

hazards.description: The outer walk's planks are rotten and slick. Weight in the wrong place can put you through the wood into the water under the pier.

onInspect.narrativeHints: Fresh gouge in the wood — something heavy dragged or braced. Points back toward Briggs's mooring, not the open bay.
The board bows. Fibers are black with rot. One hard step could open a hole to the tide.

locations.name: Under the pier — black water
locations.description: Cold, brackish, and loud with the slap of waves against piles. Kelp brushes your legs. The underside of the pier is a ribcage of wet wood. The ladder is a dark rectangle above. Low tide mud sucks at anything that stands still.

exits.label: haul yourself up the ladder to the deck

onInspect.narrativeHints: Fresh gouges at waterline height — as if a body or a boat pole slammed here hard before dawn.

relationships.label: hates being watched by the harbor
relationships.notes: Briggs resents official eyes; private behavior only until motive is known.

relationships.label: works for Briggs; resented Rourke's scolding
relationships.notes: Jess had a public row with Rourke yesterday — red herring surface.

relationships.label: town keeps her at arm's length
owes silence about night runs
relationships.notes: Private: Briggs's midnight cargo. Reveal when log scrap is known.

characters.name: Tom Rourke
characters.shortBio: Harbormaster of Coldharbor — pulled from under the pier at first light.

knowledge.public: Deceased. Recovered from the water under the pier.

characters.name: Nell Avery
characters.shortBio: Runs the fish shed; knows every boat and rumor on the pier.

knowledge.public: She opened the shed at half four. She heard a splash, then nothing. She found Rourke when the light came up and shouted for Sam.

private.content: She saw Captain Briggs leave his boat and walk toward the ladder around four. He was carrying something long under his arm. She thought it was a gaff or a pole.

secrets.content: Rourke told her last week he was done looking the other way on night boats. He did not name Briggs aloud, but she knew who he meant.

defenses: Will not invent what she did not see
Protects the working pier, not liars

characters.name: Captain Harlan Briggs
characters.shortBio: Master of the Merrow; respected, hard, always just a little too careful.
characters.voice: Gravel, practiced calm, sharp when pressed

knowledge.public: He says he slept aboard the Merrow all night. He heard nothing until Nell shouted. He and Rourke 'got on well enough.'

private.content: He and Rourke argued yesterday about 'inspections' and 'county eyes.' He calls it a professional disagreement.

defenses: Insists he never left the boat
Blames fog and the tide for confusion

characters.name: Jess Pike
characters.shortBio: Young deckhand on the Merrow; temper like a short fuse.

knowledge.public: Jess fought with Rourke yesterday over wages and 'talking back.' Everyone heard it. Jess says they slept in a loft ashore and came at dawn.

private.content: Jess can name the loft landlady who saw them until near five. Jess is scared — but of being blamed, not of the truth about four o'clock.

defenses: Will admit the row, not a killing

characters.name: Dr. Miriam Cole
characters.shortBio: Physician renting a cottage for the season; outsider with a cool eye.
characters.voice: Precise, dry, slightly weary of small towns

knowledge.public: She was walking the outer rail for air before dawn — insomnia. She heard a splash and a man's grunt, then footsteps on wood going toward the boats, not the shore.

private.content: As a doctor she will say the temple wound looks like a heavy hook or gaff strike, not a fall against the piles.

defenses: Sticks to what she heard and medically knows

characters.name: Sam Quinn
characters.shortBio: Night watch for the harbor; Rourke's friend, now a man with a dead friend.

knowledge.public: Sam walked the shore end of the pier at three and again near five. He did not walk the outer end — fog and orders. He found Nell screaming and helped haul Rourke up.

private.content: Rourke told Sam he had something for the county about night traffic. Sam wished he had asked which boat.

secrets.content: Sam always figured Briggs's 'midnight runs' were more than fish. He never had proof. The torn log would be that proof.

defenses: Grief makes him slow, not stupid

evidence.name: Rourke's smashed watch
evidence.description: Pocket watch stopped at 4:12. Glass cracked. Time of the blow or the fall into the piles.

evidence.name: Temple wound notes
evidence.description: Deep gash at the temple consistent with a heavy hook or gaff, not a simple fall.

evidence.name: Torn log scrap
evidence.description: Rourke's handwriting: 'B. midnight run confirmed. Tell county if he lies again.'

evidence.name: Bloody gaff
evidence.description: Long-handled gaff from under a tarp on the Merrow. Blood crust in the iron crease; handle wet above a normal dunk line.

beats.title: The watch stops the night
beats.narrationHints: 4:12 is a fixed point in the fog. Everything else has to hang from it.

beats.title: The letter B
beats.narrationHints: Rourke left a breadcrumb: B and a midnight run. The pier's careful manners get thinner.

reactions.lineHint: Tom said he had something for the county. I should have asked which boat.

beats.title: Iron and blood
beats.narrationHints: The weapon was never far from the Merrow. Briggs feels the net close.

reactions.lineHint: Gear gets dirty. That doesn't make it a murder weapon.

beats.title: Briggs's alibi sinks
beats.narrationHints: With the log and the gaff, Briggs's night aboard the Merrow no longer holds. He turns dangerous. A clock starts — if you stall, he may try the fog.

reactions.lineHint: You put that paper with that hook and you think you own me. Careful, inspector.

beats.title: The pier closes ranks for Jess
beats.narrationHints: Pinning the kid for a loud argument freezes half the pier. Nell especially.

beats.title: Into the fog
beats.narrationHints: You waited too long after cornering Briggs. The Merrow is gone into the fog. Failure by escape.

beats.title: High water takes the trail
beats.narrationHints: The tide returns. What the flats held is gone, and the county boat finds a colder trail. Failure by time.

beats.title: The Merrow stays at the piles
beats.narrationHints: Aftermath: Briggs on the deck under lantern light. Nell and Sam close enough to hear. Still interactive.

reactions.lineHint: He was going to the county. I told him the fog would take care of fools.

solution.summary: Captain Harlan Briggs killed Harbormaster Tom Rourke around 4:12 a.m. with a gaff after Rourke confirmed Briggs's illegal midnight cargo runs and threatened to tell the county. Briggs struck Rourke on the pier near the ladder, Rourke went into the water at low tide, and Briggs rinsed the gaff poorly and hid it under a tarp on the Merrow. He claimed he never left the boat. Nell Avery saw him leave the boat with a long tool; the torn log scrap names 'B' and the midnight run; the smashed watch fixes the time; Dr. Cole's wound reading matches a gaff.
solution.method: Gaff strike to the temple on the pier; body into the water at low tide
solution.motive: Silence Rourke before he reported smuggling / midnight runs to the county

requiredFacts.description: Identifies Captain Briggs / Harlan as the killer

timeline.event: Rourke confronts Briggs about midnight runs; threatens county report.
Public row: Jess Pike and Rourke over wages (red herring surface).
Briggs leaves Merrow with gaff, strikes Rourke near ladder, body into low-tide water.
Nell finds body; Sam helps haul Rourke up; fog holds the town.

canon.notes: Jess is the loud red herring. Cole is honest witness. Nell is key eyewitness.

endings.title: The Merrow stays tied
endings.templateNotes: Briggs is held for the county boat. The gaff, the log scrap, and the watch make a chain even fog cannot cut. Nell does not celebrate. Sam stands watch over a friend who will not stand again. Outside, the tide begins to rise — on schedule, indifferent.

endings.title: A name in the fog
endings.templateNotes: You named Briggs before every board was checked. Still, the truth lands: he cracks under the accusation, salt and guilt in equal measure. Do not invent proof the player never found. The pier listens. The Merrow does not leave.

endings.title: Enough to hold
endings.templateNotes: You have Briggs by the collar but the file is thin. He will fight the county every step. The fog does not clear — it only thins.

endings.title: Wrong fish
endings.templateNotes: You pinned the wrong name. The pier goes cold. By the time the county arrives, the real trail has washed toward open water.

endings.title: Fog takes the Merrow
endings.templateNotes: Briggs ran when he knew you had him. The Merrow is a ghost in the white. Rourke's murder leaves with him.

endings.title: Tide's in
endings.templateNotes: High water erases the flats. What you did not find is gone. The county boat finds a cleaner pier and a colder case.
